task_success_reasoning: The response delivers a complete, well-structured RFP section tailored to governance automation, using appropriate governance terminology and providing functional/non-functional requirements, acceptance criteria, deliverables, and a timeline.
retrieval_precision_reasoning: The response accurately uses governance automation terms (policy-as-code, OPA/Rego, RBAC/ABAC, SIEM, model registries) and gives concrete examples and integration points; a few performance targets were left as vendor-provided placeholders rather than specific quantified thresholds.
stale_fact_reasoning: The content is evergreen and does not rely on time-bound facts or outdated references.
irrelevant_reasoning: All sections and items are relevant to governance automation for an RFP; no extraneous topics are introduced.
knowledge_coverage_reasoning: The response covers policy lifecycle, enforcement/remediation, identity and ML governance, audit and integrations, testing, SLA and deployment options, and acceptance criteria — only minor gaps are explicit numeric performance SLAs and any Wisdm-specific constraints (no org context was provided).
